name: Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia
tagline: A strategic, remote Navy base on a coral atoll in the middle of the Indian Ocean.
_note: True OCONUS (British Indian Ocean Territory). REMOTE/UNACCOMPANIED tour. NSF Diego Garcia is a strategic US base on a UK atoll (logistics, prepositioning, bomber/ship support). Dependents do NOT relocate here -> unaccompanied/remote pattern: no family housing/OHA/DoDEA/BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy. Personnel live in on-base quarters; access by military air only. Facts verified from official sources (cnic.navy.mil NSF Diego Garcia, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Location: Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory (central Indian Ocean)
data.Role: Strategic logistics, prepositioning, and air/naval support hub
data.Access: Reached only by military air; an isolated coral atoll
data.Note: A U.S. base on UK-administered territory ('the Footprint of Freedom')

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia is a strategically vital U.S. base on a remote coral atoll in the central Indian Ocean, supporting logistics, maritime prepositioning, and air and naval operations across the region.
It's a remote, isolated tour on UK-administered territory, reached only by military aircraft, with a small, mission-focused community far from anywhere.

data.subtitle: Diego Garcia is not a family-relocation assignment. It's a remote, unaccompanied tour where personnel live in on-base quarters, so the planning is about the separation and the isolation.
intro: Because of the extreme remoteness, Diego Garcia is an unaccompanied tour. Dependents do not relocate here, so there's no family housing, OHA, BAH, or DoDEA school in the usual sense.
The real planning is for the family back home and adapting to a small, isolated island community, balanced against a tropical setting with strong morale and recreation programs.

recommendations.advice: Personnel live in on-base housing/quarters; there's no off-base community on the atoll.
Since it's unaccompanied, sort out where your family will live during the tour and your finances and communication plan early.
The base community and MWR (diving, fishing, water sports) make the isolated tour; get involved.
Remote/unaccompanied tours have their own pay and allowance rules; verify everything with finance.

sections.title: Access
data: Diego Garcia is a highly restricted, isolated installation on UK-administered territory, reached only by military air. Access is tightly controlled and coordinated through your command.
Requirements are specific; coordinate everything through your command before you travel.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base Quarters)
data: Because this is a remote, unaccompanied tour, personnel live in on-base quarters; there is no family housing or off-base community on the atoll.
Your unit handles your quarters assignment on arrival; confirm current arrangements through your sponsor and the base.

sections.title: Schools
data: Because Diego Garcia is a remote, unaccompanied tour, dependents do not relocate here, and there is no DoDEA school.
If you have school-age children, they remain at your designated location during the tour; coordinate schooling there and confirm your specific situation with your command.

data.Care: Primary and urgent care for the assigned population; complex cases require medical evacuation given the remoteness

data.Exchange: On-base Navy Exchange (NEX) for the assigned population
data.Note: Shopping is limited to what's on base; there's no local town

intro: A remote, unaccompanied island tour has its own checklist. The focus is on-base in-processing and squaring away your family's situation back home.

steps.title: Coordinate travel with your command
steps.text: Travel is by military air and tightly coordinated; confirm your flight, gear, and entitlements well in advance.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get the required ID and logistics cards.

steps.title: Get your quarters set up
steps.text: Coordinate your on-base quarters with your unit; this is your home for the tour.

steps.title: Square away the home front
steps.text: Confirm where your family lives during the tour, sort finances, allotments, powers of attorney, and a communication plan for the time apart.

steps.title: Learn the island
steps.text: Understand the limited logistics (mail, shopping, connectivity) and the MWR opportunities.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program; complex care means medevac, so understand the process.

data.q: Can my family come to Diego Garcia?
data.a: No. It's a remote, unaccompanied tour on an isolated atoll, so dependents don't relocate here, and there's no family housing or DoDEA school.

data.q: Do I get BAH or OHA?
data.a: This is an unaccompanied remote tour, so the usual family housing allowances don't apply the same way. Confirm your specific pay and allowances (and any continued BAH for family back home) with finance.
In on-base quarters. The unit handles your assignment when you arrive.
A tropical Indian Ocean setting with excellent diving, fishing, and water sports, plus a tight base community, balanced against real isolation.

sections.title: On-Base & Around
sections.note: Diego Garcia is an isolated atoll with no public town. These are on-base/area realities, not destinations to visit.

picks.name: Lagoon diving & snorkeling
picks.blurb: Clear water and reefs make diving and snorkeling a highlight.

picks.name: Base MWR & water sports
picks.blurb: Boating, fishing, and recreation programs anchor the tour.

picks.name: Atoll beaches
picks.blurb: Quiet tropical beaches around the coral atoll.

picks.name: Wildlife
picks.blurb: Sea turtles, seabirds, and coconut crabs inhabit the atoll.
